title: ADR front-matter
description: Front-matter shape for an Architecture Decision Record. Lives flat in /specs/adrs/adr-NNN-{slug}.md regardless of which scopes the decision applies to — ADRs are cross-cutting by design. Composes the universal envelope and (a) overrides status to Nygard's proposed|accepted|superseded instead of the universal draft|active|superseded, preserving the 15-year ADR community convention; (b) requires scope_tags non-empty to defuse the orphan-ADR anti-pattern; (c) tightens supersedes / superseded_by to the ADR id pattern — in practice these always cross-reference another ADR; (d) narrows recovery_status to ban AI-reconstructed content on the four human-only fields: context, alternatives_considered, rationale, consequences. Body sections (Context, Decision, Alternatives, Rationale, Consequences with its mandatory Reversibility sub-prompt) live in the markdown body and are checked by the structural/review skills, not by JSON Schema.

id.description: Stable identifier. Format ADR-{seq}-{slug}: three-or-more-digit sequence number (globally monotonic, never reused), hyphen, kebab-case slug naming the decision. Example: ADR-017-use-postgres-for-job-queue. The slug is mandatory — TARGET §5.4 treats it as load-bearing for directory navigation and human recognition.

artifact_type.description: Artifact-type discriminator, fixed for this schema.

status.description: Lifecycle state. ADR overrides the universal lifecycle vocabulary to Nygard's proposed | accepted | superseded (locked 2026-04-22 in PHASE3_AUTHORING_PATTERN §2). Once an ADR is accepted its body is immutable; changing a decision means writing a new ADR that supersedes the old one.

scope_tags.description: Scopes the decision primarily applies to. Non-empty — an ADR with no scope_tags is orphaned. An empty-string element denotes the root scope. Multiple tags are allowed for decisions that span scopes.

affected_scopes.description: Scopes the decision consequences reach beyond its primary scope. Optional; omit or leave empty when scope_tags fully covers reach.

supersedes.description: ADR id of the predecessor this ADR replaces. Narrowed from the envelope's generic artifact_id_ref to the ADR id pattern — ADR lineage crosses only to other ADRs.

superseded_by.description: ADR id of the successor that replaces this ADR. Set when status transitions to superseded.

recovery_status.description: Retrofit provenance. Narrowed for ADR: the scalar form accepts only 'verified' or 'unknown' because the ADR's core content (context, alternatives, rationale, consequences) is human-only and cannot be honestly reconstructed artifact-wide. In map form, the four named human-only keys are restricted to verified|unknown; other keys (typically 'decision' or author-added markers for observable-from-code fragments) keep the broader verified|reconstructed|unknown set.

oneOf.description: Scalar: applies to the whole ADR. 'reconstructed' banned because the artifact is human-only at its core.
Map: per-field status. Human-only keys (context, alternatives_considered, rationale, consequences) cannot be 'reconstructed'; other keys keep the full set.

human_only_recovery.description: Recovery status narrowed for ADR human-only fields. The no-fabrication rule bans 'reconstructed': AI cannot supply human rationale, context, alternatives, or anticipated consequences — those fields carry either 'verified' (a human confirmed the content) or 'unknown' (no honest record exists).
